LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ERIC AU'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION BY ERIC AU DIRECTED TO THE SECOND, FOURTH, FIFTH, SIXTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION IS GRANTED FOR THE REASONS HEREAFTER DESCRIBED. EACH OF THE DEFTS SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE BASED ON THEIR DECLARATIONS. IN RESPONSE, PLTF FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. THE COURT DECLINES TO TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE COMPLAINT FILED BY THE BANKRUPTCY TRUSTEE FOR THE CONTENTS OF THE COMPLAINT ARE INADMISSIBLE HEARSAY. THE OBJECTIONS TO THE SAMUELS' DECLARATIONS FOR LACK OF FOUNDATION ARE WELL-TAKEN. THERE IS AN ABSENCE OF FACTS PROVIDING A BASIS FOR THIS TESTIMONY AND MR. SAMUELS HAS NOT BEEN QUALIFIED AS A FORSENIC ACCOUNTING EXPERT. THE COURT DOES ADMIT THE TESTIMONY OF THE DEFTS GIVEN UNDER OATH AS A HEARSAY EXCEPTION AND SUSTAINS THE OBJECTION TO ALL OTHER DOCUMENTS ATTACHED TO THE SAMUELS' DECLARATION AS HEARSAY. THE ADMITTED TESTIMONY IS NOT SUFFICIENT TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. PLTF HAS FAILED TO PROVIDE A BASIS FOR A TRIER OF FACT TO CONCLUDE THAT DEFENDANTS CONTROLLED THE ARBITRATION DIRECTED AGAINST THE CORPORATE ENTITY OR MAY BE DEEMED THE ALTER EQO OF THE CORPORATION AND THEREFORE PERSONALLY RESPONSIBLE FOR REPRESENTATIONS MADE TO PLAINTIFF BY THE CORPORATE ENTITY OR THE BUSINESS PRACTICES OF THE CORPORATE DEFENDANT. PLTF'S REQUEST FOR A FURTHER CONTINUANCE FOR DISCOVERY HAS BEEN DENIED. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT EDWIN LAW'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. RULING:ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION BY EDWIN LAW DIRECTED TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS GRANTED FOR THE REASONS HEREAFTER DESCRIBED.EACH OF THE DEFTS SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE BASED ON THEIR DECLARATIONS.IN RESPONSE, PLTF FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT.THE COURT DECLINES TO TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE COMPLAINT FILED BY THE BANKRUPTCY TRUSTEE FOR THE CONTENTS OF THE COMPLAINT ARE INADMISSIBLE HEARSAY.THE OBJECTIONS TO THE SAMUELS' DECLARATIONS FOR LACK OF FOUNDATION ARE WELL-TAKEN.THERE IS AN ABSENCE OF FACTS PROVIDING A BASIS FOR THIS TESTIMONY AND MR. SAMUELS HAS NOT BEEN QUALIFIED AS A FORSENIC ACCOUNTING EXPERT.THE COURT DOES ADMIT THE TESTIMONY OF THE DEFENDANTS GIVEN UNDER OATH AS A HEARSAY EXCEPTION AND SUSTAINS THE OBJECTION TO ALL OTHER DOCUMENTS ATTACHED TO THE SAMUELS' DECLARATION AS HEARSAY.THE ADMITTED TESTIMONY IS NOT SUFFICIENT TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT.PLTF HAS FAILED TO PROVIDE A BASIS FOR A TRIER OF FACT TO CONCLUDE THAT DEFTS CONTROLLED THE ARBITRATION DIRECTED AGAINST THE CORPORATE ENTITY OR MAY BE DEEMED THE ALTER EQO OF THE CORPORATION AND THEREFORE PERSONALLY RESPONSIBLE FOR REPRESENTATIONS MADE TO PLTF BY THE CORPORATE ENTITY OR THE BUSINESS PRACTICES OF THE CORPORATE DEFT.PLTF'S REQUEST FOR A FURTHER CONTINUANCE FOR DISCOVERY HAS BEEN DENIED.(302/PJM)JUDGE:PATRICK J. MAHONEY,REPORTER: MARIA TORREAN

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ALICE AU'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR ALTERNATIVELY SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION/JUDGMENT BY ALICE AU DIRECTED TO THE SECOND, THIRD AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION IS GRANTED FOR THE REASONS HEREAFTER DESCRIBED. EACH OF THE DEFTS SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE BASED ON THEIR DECLARATIONS. IN RESPONSE, PLTF FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. THE COURT DECLINES TO TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE COMPLAINT FILED BY THE BANKRUPTCY TRUSTEE FOR THE CONTENTS OF THE COMPLAINT ARE INADMISSIBLE HEARSAY. THE OBJECTIONS TO THE SAMUELS' DECLARATIONS FOR LACK OF FOUNDATION ARE WELL-TAKEN. THERE IS AN ABSENCE OF FACTS PROVIDING A BASIS FOR THIS TESTIMONY AND MR. SAMUELS HAS NOT BEEN QUALIFIED AS A FORSENIC ACCOUNTING EXPERT.THE COURT DOES ADMIT THE TESTIMONY OF THE DEFTS GIVEN UNDER OATH AS A HEARSAY EXCEPTION AND SUSTAINS THE OBJECTION TO ALL OTHER DOCUMENTS ATTACHED TO THE SAMUELS' DECLARATION AS HEARSAY.THE ADMITTED TESTIMONY IS NOT SUFFICIENT TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT.PLTF HAS FAILED TO PROVIDE A BASIS FOR A TRIER OF FACT TO CONCLUDE THAT DEFTS CONTROLLED THE ARBITRATION DIRECTED AGAINST THE CORPORATE ENTITY OR MAY BE DEEMED THE ALTER EQO OF THE CORPORATION AND THEREFORE PERSONALLY RESPONSIBLE FOR REPRESENTATIONS MADE TO PLTF BY THE CORPORATE ENTITY OR THE BUSINESS PRACTICES OF THE CORPORATE DEFT.PLTF'S REQUEST FOR A FURTHER CONTINUANCE FOR DISCOVERY HAS BEEN DENIED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-07-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ERIC AU'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
APPEARANCES: Kenneth C. Greene, Esq. of Hamrick & Evans, LLP 818/763-5292 for pltfs & opposing parties; Clyde I. Butts, Esq. of Law Offices of Clyde I. Butts 925/943-1850 for deft Gavin Lam; Mark M. O'Brien, Esq. of Mark M. O'Brien A Law Corporation 626/301-1164 for defts Eric Au and Alice Au; Rodney A. Marraccini, Esq. of Law Offices of Rodney A. Marraccini 925/943-1850 for deft Edwin Law.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - The motion for summary adjudication by Eric Au directed to the second, fourth, fifth, sixth and seventh causes of action is granted for the reasons hereafter described. Each of the defts shifted the burden of producing evidence based on their declarations. In response, pltf failed to present evidence that raised a triable issue of fact. The Court declines to take judicial notice of the complaint filed by the bankruptcy trustee for the contents of the complaint are inadmissible hearsay. The objections to the Samuels' declarations for lack of foundation are well-taken. There is an absence of facts providing a basis for this testimony and Mr. Samuels has not been qualified as a forsenic accounting expert. The Court does admit the testimony of the defts given under oath as a hearsay exception and sustains the objection to all other documents attached to the Samuels' declaration as hearsay. The admitted testimony is not sufficient to create a triable issue of fact. Pltf has failed to provide a basis for a trier of fact to conclude that defendants controlled the arbitration directed against the corporate entity or may be deemed the alter eqo of the corporation and therefore personally responsible for representations made to plaintiff by the corporate entity or the business practices of the corporate defendant. Pltf's request for a further continuance for discovery has been denied. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
